doc_title: Illinois Forcible Entry and Detainer Act — 5-Day Notice
label: partial
reason: The 5-day notice requirement and basic service methods reflect genuine provisions of 735 ILCS 5/9-209, but the email service provision and the "conformed text 2024" framing appear to be fabricated additions not found in the actual statute.
response_turn1:
LABEL: PARTIAL
CONFIDENCE: 72
REASON: The core 5-day notice requirement and much of the language from 735 ILCS 5/9-209 is genuine, but the document adds fabricated or unverifiable elements—particularly the claim about email service with written tenant consent, which does not appear in the actual statute—and presents itself as a "conformed text" in a reformatted style that is not an official publication.
response_turn2:
LABEL: PARTIAL
CONFIDENCE: 74
REASON: The 5-day notice requirement and basic service methods reflect genuine provisions of 735 ILCS 5/9-209, but the email service provision and the "conformed text 2024" framing appear to be fabricated additions not found in the actual statute.
